questionText: A toy train adventure must finish exactly at 4:25 p.m. It has 36 minutes of tunnel time, then a 14-minute snack stop, then 48 minutes of station games. One plan says the adventure should start at 2:47 p.m. Choose the best answer about that plan.
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Time Problem Solving: Duration & Schedules, aligned to Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes.
options: [{"text":"The plan is correct because 36 + 14 + 48 = 98 minutes, and 98 minutes before 4:25 p.m. is 2:47 p.m.","correct":true},{"text":"The plan is not correct because the adventure should start at 3:07 p.m.","correct":false},{"text":"The plan is not correct because the adventure should start at 2:57 p.m.","correct":false},{"text":"The plan is not correct because the adventure lasts only 88 minutes.","correct":false}]
explanation: Nice thinking! Add the times first: 36 + 14 + 48 = 98 minutes, which is 1 hour 38 minutes. Count back 1 hour 38 minutes from 4:25 p.m. to get 2:47 p.m. The 3:07 and 2:57 answers come from counting back wrongly, and 88 minutes is a adding mistake.
generationMetadata: {"questionNumber":"Q1","questionType":"mcq","subject":"Maths","grade":4,"topic":"Time Problem Solving","subtopic":"Duration & Schedules","learning_objective":"Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes","focus":"reverse_reasoning"}

questionText: A panda cartoon marathon ends at 4:18 p.m. It lasted 2 hours 27 minutes in all. There was a 9-minute pause in the middle, and that pause is included in the 2 hours 27 minutes. Which student found the correct start time?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Time Problem Solving: Duration & Schedules, aligned to Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes.
options: [{"text":"Ari says 1:42 p.m. because she subtracted 2 hours 27 minutes and then also subtracted the 9-minute pause.","correct":false},{"text":"Bea says 1:51 p.m. because 4:18 p.m. − 2 hours = 2:18 p.m., and 2:18 p.m. − 27 minutes = 1:51 p.m.","correct":true},{"text":"Chen says 2:00 p.m. because he subtracted 18 minutes to get 4:00 p.m. and then 2 hours.","correct":false},{"text":"Drew says 2:09 p.m. because he subtracted the 9-minute pause only once and forgot the rest of the minutes.","correct":false}]
explanation: Bea is correct. Since the 9-minute pause is already included in the total 2 hours 27 minutes, do not subtract it again. Work backward from 4:18 p.m.: subtract 2 hours to get 2:18 p.m., then subtract 27 minutes to get 1:51 p.m. Ari’s answer is too early because she removed the pause twice. Chen and Drew did not subtract the full elapsed time.

questionText: Choose the method that correctly solves this snack-shop puzzle. The jellybean stall opens at 11:54 a.m. It sells sweets for 28 minutes, closes for a 17-minute tidy-up, then sells sweets again for 46 minutes. Pick the correct finish time for the whole schedule.
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Time Problem Solving: Duration & Schedules, aligned to Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes.
options: [{"text":"Add 28 + 17 + 46 = 91 minutes, so the stall finishes at 1:25 p.m.","correct":true},{"text":"Add only the selling times, 28 + 46 = 74 minutes, so the stall finishes at 1:08 p.m.","correct":false},{"text":"Add 28 + 17 = 45 minutes, so the stall finishes at 12:39 p.m.","correct":false},{"text":"Add 28 + 17 + 46 = 91 minutes, but say 11:54 a.m. + 91 minutes = 1:35 p.m.","correct":false}]
explanation: The whole schedule includes all three parts: 28 minutes of selling, 17 minutes of tidy-up, and 46 more minutes of selling. So the total is 28 + 17 + 46 = 91 minutes. Starting at 11:54 a.m., 6 minutes gets to 12:00 p.m., leaving 85 minutes. Then 60 more minutes gets to 1:00 p.m., leaving 25 minutes. So the finish time is 1:25 p.m. The second option forgets the tidy-up, the third stops too early, and the fourth adds the total minutes but miscalculates the ending time.

questionText: A sticker treasure trail starts at 1:37 p.m. The trail takes 1 hour 48 minutes. The map says everyone must stop for a 12-minute water break, and that break is already included in the 1 hour 48 minutes. Three students gave answers. Which answer is correct?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Time Problem Solving: Duration & Schedules, aligned to Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes.
options: [{"text":"3:13 p.m., because the 12-minute break should be subtracted from 1 hour 48 minutes.","correct":false},{"text":"3:25 p.m., because 1:37 p.m. + 1 hour = 2:37 p.m., and 2:37 p.m. + 48 minutes = 3:25 p.m.","correct":true},{"text":"3:37 p.m., because the 12-minute break should be added after 1 hour 48 minutes.","correct":false},{"text":"4:25 p.m., because 48 minutes is almost another full hour after 2:37 p.m.","correct":false}]
explanation: The correct ending time is 3:25 p.m. Add the given trail time only once: 1:37 p.m. + 1 hour = 2:37 p.m., and 2:37 p.m. + 48 minutes = 3:25 p.m. The water break is already part of the 1 hour 48 minutes, so it should not be added again or subtracted out. That is why 3:13 p.m. and 3:37 p.m. are incorrect. 4:25 p.m. is much too late because 48 minutes is less than 1 hour.

questionText: The slide game runs from 2:26 p.m. to 3:11 p.m. The swing game runs from 4:48 p.m. to 5:40 p.m. Which claim correctly compares the two game times and explains the difference?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Time Problem Solving: Duration & Schedules, aligned to Students can calculate elapsed time across hours and minutes.
options: [{"text":"The swing game is longer by 7 minutes because the slide game lasts 45 minutes and the swing game lasts 52 minutes.","correct":true},{"text":"The slide game is longer by 7 minutes because it starts earlier in the day.","correct":false},{"text":"Both games last 52 minutes because each one ends in the next hour.","correct":false},{"text":"The swing game is longer by 17 minutes because the slide game lasts 35 minutes and the swing game lasts 52 minutes.","correct":false}]
explanation: The slide game lasts from 2:26 p.m. to 3:11 p.m. That is 34 minutes to 3:00 p.m. and 11 more minutes, so 45 minutes total. The swing game lasts from 4:48 p.m. to 5:40 p.m. That is 12 minutes to 5:00 p.m. and 40 more minutes, so 52 minutes total. Since 52 − 45 = 7, the swing game is longer by 7 minutes. Starting earlier in the day does not change duration, and the other claims use incorrect elapsed times.
